title: Velvety Fermented Japanese Yellow Pepper Soup
description: A lowsodium, velvety Japanese soup that combines roasted yellow peppers with fermented miso and kombu for deep umami, perfect for a solo dinner.

ingredients.name: Dried shiitake mushrooms
ingredients.notes: reconstituted in warm water for 20 min

instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Halve the yellow bell peppers, remove seeds, and place cut-side down on a baking sheet.
Roast the peppers for 20 minutes until skins darken and flesh softens. Transfer to a bowl, cover, and let steam for 10 minutes to loosen skins.
Meanwhile, soak the kombu and shiitake mushrooms in 1 cup of warm water for 20 minutes. Drain the kombu, remove, and set aside.
In a saucepan, combine the water, vegetable stock, reconstituted shiitake, kombu, and miso paste. Simmer gently for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally to dissolve the miso.
Add the roasted pepper flesh, ginger, garlic, and soy sauce to the broth. Continue simmering for 10 minutes to meld flavors.
Remove the kombu from the pot, as it can impart bitterness if left too long. Adjust seasoning with a pinch of salt if needed.
Stir in sesame oil and rice vinegar. Let the soup finish cooking for a final 3 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with sliced green onions. For extra velvety texture, briefly pulse the soup in a blender before serving.
Enjoy your low-sodium, fermented Japanese yellow pepper soup as a comforting solo dinner.
